title: ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – JPO 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands | Salary €71,173 Per Year
summary: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has officially opened applications for the prestigious Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme 2026 sponsored by the Republic of Korea. This highly competitive international opportunity offers young Korean professionals the chance to work at the ICC headquarters in The Hague, Netherlands, while contributing to global justice and international institutional management. The position is hosted within the Human Resources Section of the ICC Registry and provides selected candidates with hands-on experience in strategic HR management, organizational development, policy implementation, workforce planning, recruitment operations, learning and development, and international administration. The application deadline is 26 June 2026 (midnight The Hague time). About the International Criminal Court (ICC) The International Criminal Court is one of the world’s leading international judicial institutions, responsible for investigating and prosecuting individuals accused of genocide, crimes against humanity, war crimes, and crimes of aggression. Headquartered in The Hague, Netherlands, the ICC operates with professionals from diverse cultural and professional backgrounds. The organization provides an international working environment focused on justice, accountability, professionalism, diversity, and global cooperation. The Human Resources Section plays a critical role in supporting the Court’s global mission by ensuring effective workforce management, organizational efficiency, strategic talent development, and compliance with international administrative standards. Overview of the JPO Position Position Title Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Duty Station The Hague, Netherlands Organization International Criminal Court (ICC) Organizational Unit Human Resources Section, Division of Management Services, Registry Contract Duration One-year appointment with possibility of extension according to the JPO Memorandum of Understanding Annual Salary Minimum Net Annual Salary (including post adjustment): €71,173 Application Deadline 26 June 2026 (midnight The Hague time) What Makes This Opportunity Significant? This JPO role offers early-career professionals the rare opportunity to: Work directly within a major international organization Gain exposure to UN Common System HR frameworks Participate in strategic HR and organizational development projects Build expertise in international recruitment and policy implementation Contribute to organizational transformation initiatives Develop experience in multicultural and multinational workplace management Work alongside global experts in administration, HR operations, and institutional governance For candidates interested in long-term careers with the United Nations system, international NGOs, global institutions, or diplomatic organizations, this opportunity provides outstanding career-building exposure. Human Resources Section at the ICC The Human Resources Section provides centralized HR advisory, administrative, and strategic support across the Court. It operates within the Division of Management Services alongside: Budget Section Finance Section Safety and Security Section General Services Section Occupational Health Unit SAP Competence Centre The HR Section focuses on both operational and strategic human resource management functions. Key Areas Managed by the HR Section The section oversees: Recruitment and onboarding Workforce planning Job design and classification Payroll administration Employee benefits and entitlements Pension and insurance management Performance management Organizational development Learning and development programmes Career planning and succession management Staff relations and grievance support Structure of the Human Resources Section The HR Section consists of two specialized units: 1. HR Operations Unit This unit handles operational HR functions including: Recruitment processes Staff onboarding Compensation administration Payroll management Benefits and entitlements Post management Health insurance administration Pension administration HR-related staff complaints and grievances Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) staff data management 2. HR Organizational Development Unit This unit focuses on strategic HR initiatives such as: Workforce planning Talent management Organizational design Staff development Training programmes Learning and development strategies Performance management systems Leadership and managerial development Duties and Responsibilities Under the supervision of the Head of the Human Resources Operations Unit, the selected JPO will support multiple HR functions and strategic projects. Main Responsibilities Include: Supporting implementation of HR-related projects and strategic initiatives Conducting research on HR policies, procedures, and best practices from comparable international organizations Assisting in the development of HR guidelines and institutional procedures Liaising with HR specialists on recruitment, entitlements, insurance, policy interpretation, and administrative matters Reviewing HR issues and presenting analytical recommendations to management Supporting the monitoring and evaluation of HR policies and organizational practices Assisting in the development of HR performance indicators and management tools Contributing to Service Level Agreements and HR work plan tracking systems Conducting preliminary policy analysis and assisting in drafting position papers Designing internal communication materials supporting HR objectives and institutional goals Providing support across HR units whenever required Performing additional duties assigned by HR leadership Required Qualifications Educational Requirements Applicants must possess either: An advanced university degree in: Human Resources Management Business Administration Public Administration Law Social Sciences Information Management Or another related field OR A first-level university degree combined with two additional years of qualifying experience Professional Experience Requirements Candidates must have: Minimum two years of relevant HR experience OR Four years of experience if applying with a first-level university degree Desirable Experience Additional advantages include experience in: International organizations UN Common System salary and allowance structures Broad HR operations and administration HR policy implementation Organizational development initiatives Required Skills and Competencies The ICC seeks candidates with strong technical and interpersonal competencies. Important Skills Include: Human resources management knowledge Strong analytical and research skills Strategic planning capabilities Results-oriented work approach Ability to work under pressure and tight deadlines Excellent communication skills Professional writing ability Cross-cultural collaboration skills Confidentiality and discretion Flexibility and motivation Strong professional integrity Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate: Fluency in English or French Knowledge of the second working language is considered desirable. Additional knowledge of official ICC languages such as: Arabic Chinese Russian Spanish will be considered an advantage. Eligibility Requirements for Republic of Korea JPO Applicants This position is specifically sponsored by the Republic of Korea and therefore includes additional eligibility conditions. Applicants must: Be Korean citizens Be fluent in Korean and English Hold at least a Bachelor’s degree Meet English proficiency requirements Be under 32 years old as of 31 December 2026 Have completed or be exempt from Korean military service (for male applicants) Accepted English Test Scores Applicants must provide valid English test results meeting at least one of the following minimum scores: TOEFL iBT: 100 TEPS: 430 IELTS: 7 TOEIC: 900 Additional notes: TOEFL Home Edition is accepted TOEFL MyBest Scores are not accepted IELTS Academic and General Training are both accepted Scores remain valid for two years after the test date Military Service Age Extension The maximum age limit may be extended for applicants who completed Korean military service: One-year extension for service under one year Two-year extension for service between one and two years Three-year extension for service of two years or more Required Documents Applicants must submit all documents in English through the ICC e-recruitment system. Required Documents Include: Resident Register issued by the Korean Government University degree certificates English proficiency certificate Employment certificates detailing: Employment period Organization Department Position title Duties and responsibilities Military service certificate (if applicable) National Basic Livelihood Security certificate (for low-income applicants) Disability certificate (for applicants with disabilities) Why You Should Apply This ICC JPO opportunity offers substantial professional and international career benefits, including: International HR experience Exposure to global institutional operations High-level policy and organizational development work Competitive international salary Multicultural professional environment Networking with global professionals Career pathway into international organizations and the UN system Professional growth in strategic HR management Final Thoughts The ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) JPO Programme 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious Korean professionals seeking international exposure in human resources management and organizational development. Working at the International Criminal Court provides not only a competitive international career pathway but also the opportunity to contribute to one of the world’s most significant global justice institutions. Candidates with strong HR expertise, analytical capabilities, international outlook, and commitment to public service are strongly encouraged to apply before the 26 June 2026 deadline. How to Apply Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE . Click HERE for more information about the ICC Associate Human Resources Officer job opportunity. Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here . The post ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – JPO 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands | Salary €71,173 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Bombshell Impact Design Agency Launches the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 — Win Up to $10,000 in Design, Marketing & AI Services
summary: Applications are now officially open for the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 by Bombshell Impact Design Agency, an award-winning social impact branding and design agency recognized as the Best Social Impact Branding Design Agency 2026 by the Corporate Vision Canadian Business Awards. This global initiative is designed to empower social impact organizations that are creating meaningful change in communities around the world. Through this grant, selected organizations will receive up to $10,000 USD worth of professional design, marketing, branding, and AI-powered business support services to help amplify their mission and increase visibility. The application deadline is August 19, 2026, in recognition of World Humanitarian Day. About the “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant The “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant is more than a funding opportunity. It is a visibility and growth initiative created specifically for organizations that are driving social transformation but may lack the branding, communication, or digital infrastructure needed to expand their reach and impact. Bombshell Impact Design Agency believes that many world-changing missions remain unseen not because of lack of value, but because of limited visibility, storytelling, and strategic positioning. This initiative aims to bridge that gap by helping impactful organizations strengthen how they communicate and present their mission to the world. The program welcomes applications from organizations across every region globally, reinforcing the agency’s commitment to supporting changemakers regardless of geographic location. Grant Benefits Selected recipients can receive up to $10,000 USD in professional services, including: Branding and identity development Strategic marketing support AI-powered creative and communication services Digital visibility enhancement Storytelling and campaign support Design solutions tailored to mission-driven organizations Strategic impact communication support These services are intended to help organizations improve outreach, attract supporters, strengthen campaigns, and scale their impact more effectively. Why This Opportunity Matters Many social impact organizations struggle with limited resources for branding, communication, and digital marketing. While their missions may be transformative, they often face challenges in: Building public awareness Reaching donors and supporters Creating compelling digital campaigns Developing strong visual identities Competing for visibility online Communicating measurable impact effectively This grant directly addresses those challenges by providing expert-level creative and strategic services that many organizations would otherwise struggle to afford. Who Can Apply? The opportunity is open to social impact organizations worldwide that are actively working to create positive change in areas such as: Sustainability Environmental protection Community development Humanitarian support Education Accessibility Social justice Health and wellbeing Youth empowerment Innovation for social good Organizations of different sizes and stages are encouraged to apply, especially those with compelling missions and measurable impact stories. How to Apply Applicants must complete a simple multi-step application process. Step 1: Complete the Application Form Organizations are required to provide information about: Their mission Their organization The impact they are creating Their long-term goals The communities they serve Applicants should clearly explain how the grant support would help expand or strengthen their work. Step 2: Upload a Short Video Applicants must submit a 30–60 second video filmed on a phone or simple camera setup. The video should: Introduce the organization Share the mission and impact story Explain why the work matters Highlight the organization’s vision for change The agency specifically encourages applicants to share their story authentically and in their own voice. Step 3: Application Review and Winner Selection After the deadline closes, all applications will be reviewed carefully by the Bombshell Impact Design Agency team. Selected winners will then be announced following the evaluation process. Important Deadline Application Deadline: August 19, 2026 The deadline coincides with World Humanitarian Day, emphasizing the global importance of humanitarian and social impact initiatives. Applicants are encouraged to apply early and prepare a compelling story-driven application. A Global Opportunity for Changemakers One of the strongest aspects of this initiative is its international accessibility. Organizations from every continent are eligible to apply, making this a truly global social impact opportunity. Whether an organization is based in Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, Oceania, or the Middle East, they are encouraged to submit their application if they are making meaningful contributions toward positive change. The Core Message Behind the Initiative The campaign carries a powerful message: “The sky was never the limit. Visibility was.” This reflects the idea that many organizations already possess the passion, innovation, and commitment needed to change the world. What they often need most is strategic visibility and stronger communication support to reach larger audiences and unlock greater opportunities. Why Organizations Should Apply This grant presents a unique opportunity for social impact organizations to gain access to high-value professional services without direct financial cost. Organizations should consider applying because it offers: International visibility Access to expert branding support Professional marketing services AI-driven creative solutions Increased awareness opportunities Stronger communication tools Enhanced storytelling capacity Strategic mission amplification For organizations seeking growth, awareness, and stronger public engagement, this initiative could become a major catalyst for expansion and long-term impact. Final Thoughts The Bombshell Impact Design Agency Social Impact Grant 2026 stands out as an innovative and mission-driven opportunity for organizations working toward meaningful global change. Rather than focusing solely on financial funding, the initiative addresses one of the biggest modern challenges facing nonprofits and social enterprises: visibility. For organizations with transformative missions but limited branding or marketing capacity, this grant could provide the professional support needed to elevate their message, strengthen their outreach, and expand their influence globally. Interested organizations should begin preparing their applications, impact narratives, and short video submissions before the August 19, 2026 deadline. Learn more and apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE .
