title: Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 in Birmingham: A Unique Opportunity for Future Software Engineers
summary: Students interested in software engineering, technology, and innovation now have an exciting opportunity to gain firsthand exposure to one of the world s leading financial institutions. The Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 is officially accepting applications from students who are eager to explore careers in engineering, strengthen their technical abilities, and build meaningful professional networks. Designed specifically for students in their penultimate or final year of study, the Engineering Academy is a highly interactive two-day insight program that introduces participants to the world of software engineering at Goldman Sachs. About the Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy The Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy is an immersive learning experience aimed at helping students better understand the role of engineering within the organization and the broader technology industry. The program is open to students from all academic disciplines who have an interest in programming, technology, and using logical thinking to build scalable software solutions and systems. Through interactive workshops, networking sessions, and collaborative activities, participants will gain valuable exposure to engineering careers while developing skills that can support future applications for software engineering opportunities. Why Engineering Matters at Goldman Sachs Engineering sits at the heart of Goldman Sachs global operations. The firm s engineers develop innovative technologies that power businesses, financial markets, and client solutions around the world. They combine cutting-edge technologies, open-source software, cloud computing solutions, databases, and programming languages to create applications that are accurate, scalable, and impactful. Software engineers at Goldman Sachs work on complex challenges that require creativity, technical expertise, and collaboration. The Engineering Academy offers participants a chance to learn how these professionals contribute to solving real-world business problems through technology. What Participants Will Gain Students selected for the program will have the opportunity to: 1. Explore Engineering Career Opportunities Participants will discover the wide range of career paths available within software engineering and technology at Goldman Sachs. 2. Develop Technical Skills The program includes practical workshops and collaborative activities designed to strengthen technical knowledge and problem-solving abilities. 3. Build Soft Skills In addition to technical development, students will improve essential professional skills such as: Communication Teamwork Leadership Collaboration Critical thinking Professional networking 4. Learn from Industry Professionals Participants will interact directly with Goldman Sachs professionals and gain insights into their career journeys, experiences, and diverse backgrounds. 5. Expand Professional Networks Students will connect with peers who share similar interests while building relationships with professionals working across engineering and technology teams. 6. Experience Goldman Sachs Culture The program provides valuable insight into Goldman Sachs long-standing apprenticeship culture and commitment to professional growth and excellence. Program Location The 2026 Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy will take place in: Birmingham, United Kingdom The program will run over two days in September 2026. Eligibility Requirements To be considered for the Engineering Academy, applicants must: Be enrolled in a Bachelor s or Master s degree program. Be graduating in 2027 or June 2028. Have an interest in software engineering, programming, technology, and problem-solving. Be interested in learning how engineering supports innovation within financial services and technology. Importantly, the program welcomes students from all academic backgrounds, making it accessible to individuals who may not be pursuing traditional computer science or engineering degrees. What Makes This Opportunity Valuable? The Engineering Academy offers more than just a short learning experience. It serves as a platform for students to: Explore future software engineering careers. Gain exposure to a global financial institution. Strengthen both technical and professional competencies. Learn directly from experienced engineers. Build a network of peers and industry professionals. Better understand the skills needed to succeed in technology-focused careers. For students considering a future in software engineering or technology, the program provides a valuable opportunity to gain practical insights before graduation. Application Timeline Application Deadline 21 June 2026 Notification Period Applicants can expect to hear back from Goldman Sachs by July 2026 . Programme Dates The Engineering Academy will take place over two days in September 2026 . How to Apply Interested students should submit their applications through Goldman Sachs Careers. Students are encouraged to apply as early as possible before the deadline. Apply Here Final Thoughts The Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for students to gain exposure to software engineering careers, strengthen their technical and professional skills, and connect with industry experts. Whether you come from a technical or non-technical academic background, this programme offers a valuable introduction to the engineering teams that drive innovation across one of the world s most influential organizations. If you are graduating in 2027 or June 2028 and have an interest in technology, software development, and problem-solving, this programme could be an important step toward your future career. For more information on this opportunity visit here Discover more opportunities on OFY, here The post Goldman Sachs EMEA Engineering Academy 2026 in Birmingham: A Unique Opportunity for Future Software Engineers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Stellenbosch University MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair Scholarships 2027: Fully Funded Opportunities for Emerging Researcher
summary: The South African Research Chair in Violent Histories and Transgenerational Trauma (SARChI Chair), based at the Centre for the Study of the Afterlife of Violence and the Reparative Quest (AVReQ) at Stellenbosch University, has opened applications for its 2027 MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair Scholarships. Funded by South Africa’s National Research Foundation (NRF), these prestigious scholarships support academically outstanding students who wish to pursue advanced research into the enduring legacies of violence, trauma, memory, healing, and social repair. The programme provides a unique opportunity for emerging scholars to engage with critical questions surrounding violent histories, transgenerational trauma, psychosocial healing, and decolonial knowledge production within a collaborative and interdisciplinary academic environment. About the MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair The MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair is a specialized postgraduate programme designed to equip scholars with the theoretical, methodological, and practical tools necessary to examine the complex and long-lasting consequences of violence across generations. The programme focuses on: Decolonial approaches to knowledge production Violent histories and their continuing social impacts Transgenerational trauma and memory Psychosocial processes of healing and repair Social justice and restorative practices Community-based and collaborative research Public engagement and intellectual contribution Students will become part of the vibrant intellectual community at AVReQ, engaging with scholars, researchers, and practitioners working on some of the most pressing questions related to violence, justice, memory, and healing. Scholarship Benefits The scholarship supports full-time enrolment in the MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair programme at Stellenbosch University. Scholarship Duration Two years of funded study Programme Start Date January 2027 Funding Structure South African citizens and permanent residents may receive full NRF scholarships. International students who are selected may receive partial scholarship funding. Eligibility Requirements To be considered for the scholarship, applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements: Academic Requirements Applicants must: Hold an Honours degree or equivalent qualification before the MPhil programme begins. Have achieved a minimum final mark of 65% in their Honours degree. Have completed their Honours degree no earlier than 2024. Be able to provide proof of graduation or confirmation from a supervisor indicating expected graduation by the end of the 2026 academic year. Research and Personal Commitment Applicants must also demonstrate: A strong commitment to postgraduate education and research. A genuine interest in exploring issues related to violent histories, trauma, memory, justice, healing, and repair. A willingness to contribute actively to a collaborative research community. Intellectual curiosity and engagement with decolonial scholarship. Required Application Documents Applicants must first apply for admission to the MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair through Stellenbosch University s online application system. In addition, candidates must submit a separate scholarship application by email. The scholarship application package must include: 1. Academic Qualification Documents Copy of Honours degree certificate, OR Letter from a supervisor confirming expected graduation by the end of 2026 2. Sample of Academic Work Applicants should provide one example of previous academic writing, such as: Academic essay Journal publication Dissertation chapter Honours research project chapter 3. Scholarship Motivation Letter (Maximum Three Pages) The motivation letter should address: Academic background and achievements Leadership experience and community engagement Professional, civic, or academic involvement Research interests and alignment with the programme Intended contribution to AVReQ s intellectual and public life Financial need (where applicable) 4. Recommendation Letter Applicants must submit: At least one recommendation letter from an academic mentor familiar with their work and capabilities 5. Declaration Form Candidates must complete and submit: Declaration of Authorship, Generative AI Use, and Writing Assistance Form Submission Process All scholarship materials should be submitted via email to: AVREQINFO@sun.ac.za Use the following email subject line: MPHIL SCHOLARSHIP APPLICATION 2027 Application Deadline All applications must be received by: Monday, 22 June 2026 at 12:00 PM (Noon) Late applications are unlikely to be considered. Interview Process Shortlisted candidates will be invited to participate in interviews. Interview Date 25 June 2026 Applicants should ensure they are available on this date if shortlisted. Why Apply? This scholarship offers a rare opportunity to study one of the most urgent and relevant areas of contemporary scholarship. Successful applicants will benefit from: NRF-funded postgraduate support Access to leading scholars and researchers Participation in a dynamic interdisciplinary research community Training in decolonial and reparative scholarship Opportunities to contribute to public discourse and social transformation Engagement with critical questions surrounding trauma, healing, justice, and memory For students committed to understanding and addressing the enduring effects of violence and historical injustice, the MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair provides an exceptional platform for academic growth, research excellence, and meaningful societal impact. For more information on this scholarship visit here Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY here The post Stellenbosch University MPhil in Violent Histories and Repair Scholarships 2027: Fully Funded Opportunities for Emerging Researcher appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Dalberg Research Fellow (12-Month Fellowship): Global Development Research Opportunity in India with Remote/Hybrid Flexibility
summary: The Dalberg Research Fellow 2026 is a prestigious 12-month fellowship offered by Dalberg Advisors , a globally recognized impact consulting firm working at the intersection of strategy, evidence, design, and systems change. This opportunity is designed for experienced researchers, policy professionals, and development practitioners who want to shape the future of global development through high-impact research, thought leadership, and innovation. Based primarily in India , with remote and hybrid working options available , this fellowship provides a rare opportunity to contribute to a newly emerging research and thought leadership initiative within Dalberg focused on shaping global development agendas. The fellowship is part of Dalberg’s effort to establish a forward-looking research engine that generates insights on major global challenges such as climate finance, gender equity, education systems, AI in development, and inclusive economic growth. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis , with a preferred start date from June 2026 onward . About Dalberg and Its Global Mission Dalberg Advisors is part of the broader Dalberg Group, a global organization committed to building a more inclusive and sustainable world. With 28 offices worldwide and experience in over 135 countries , Dalberg integrates: Strategic advisory expertise Rigorous research and data analysis Deep local and regional knowledge Cross-sector collaboration with governments, NGOs, and private sector actors Dalberg operates at the intersection of: Strategy and policy design Evidence-based development research Systems transformation and innovation Global impact consulting The organization works across major global development themes including education, gender equity, climate change, digital infrastructure, and economic justice. About the Research Thought Leadership Initiative The Research Fellow will contribute to a newly established Dalberg Observatory-style initiative , designed to produce forward-thinking research that shapes global development discourse. This initiative focuses on generating research and insights in emerging global areas such as: Future of outcomes-based funding and philanthropic capital flows Systems change measurement and collective impact evaluation Gender equity challenges, including boys’ education and underexplored inequalities Artificial intelligence in education, work, and public services Future of development financing and global policy innovation The initiative aims to produce high-quality research publications, convenings, and thought leadership that influence policymakers, donors, and global institutions. Key Objectives of the Research Fellow Role The Research Fellow will play a central role in shaping and executing Dalberg’s research agenda by: Leading or supporting original research on global development themes Contributing to quarterly publications and research outputs Translating internal consulting insights into global thought leadership Developing policy-relevant research products and analytical frameworks Supporting experimental research design and foresight analysis Key thematic focus areas include: Global development funding flows (climate, education, gender) Systems change metrics and impact measurement frameworks Education equity, especially boys’ education AI applications in governance, public services, and labor markets Key Responsibilities The Research Fellow will engage in a wide range of analytical and strategic tasks, including: 1. Research and Analysis Conduct structured desk research and literature reviews Perform qualitative and quantitative analysis Build comparative case studies across regions and sectors Support data synthesis and interpretation 2. Writing and Publication Draft research briefs, memos, blogs, and analytical papers Develop thought leadership content for internal and external audiences Contribute to quarterly publications and research outputs 3. Data Visualization and Insight Generation Support development of data visualization products Translate complex datasets into accessible insights Assist in producing research presentations and reports 4. Stakeholder Engagement and Collaboration Engage with internal Dalberg teams globally Support research partnerships and roundtable discussions Assist in designing events and convenings on key topics 5. Institutional Development Contribute to shaping the research initiative’s structure Support operational design and strategic development Help define long-term research direction and outputs Desired Qualifications and Experience Candidates are expected to bring a strong mix of analytical ability, research experience, and intellectual curiosity. Required Qualifications 4–8 years of experience in global development, research, or public policy Master’s degree preferred in fields such as: Public Policy International Affairs Sociology Public Health Data Journalism or related disciplines Technical Skills Strong writing, synthesis, and communication skills Ability to translate complex ideas into accessible insights Experience with at least one statistical tool: Python R Stata SPSS Optional but valuable: GIS mapping tools Qualitative analysis software (NVivo, ATLAS.ti, MAXQDA) Professional Experience Experience in think tanks, research institutes, or policy organizations Familiarity with development finance or global policy systems Exposure to consulting or advisory environments is an advantage Personal Attributes Strong intellectual curiosity and independent thinking Ability to work in ambiguous and evolving research environments Capacity to produce rapid, high-quality research outputs Self-motivated and impact-driven mindset What This Fellowship Offers The Dalberg Research Fellow role is designed as both a professional development opportunity and a potential long-term career pathway. Participants will benefit from: Opportunity to publish high-impact global development research Mentorship from senior global experts Exposure to international policy ecosystems Access to high-level development networks Participation in shaping a new research institution within Dalberg Possibility of transitioning into a long-term leadership role High-trust environment focused on innovation and intellectual freedom This fellowship is ideal for professionals seeking to move into high-level research leadership, policy advisory roles, or global development strategy positions. Application Timeline Applications: Rolling basis Preferred Start Date: From June 2026 Duration: 12 months (full-time fellowship) Location: India (Remote/Hybrid options available) Early application is strongly encouraged due to limited placement capacity and continuous review. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply directly through Dalberg Advisors’ official careers or recruitment channels , following instructions for the Research Fellow position. Apply Here Why This Opportunity Matters The Dalberg Research Fellow role is more than a fellowship—it is a launchpad into the future of global development thinking. It offers participants the chance to influence how governments, donors, and global institutions approach complex challenges such as inequality, climate change, education reform, and digital transformation. For researchers ready to move beyond traditional analysis and contribute to systems-level change, this fellowship provides a rare opportunity to shape global narratives and policy innovation at scale. Discover more global fellowship positions on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Dalberg Research Fellow (12-Month Fellowship): Global Development Research Opportunity in India with Remote/Hybrid Flexibility appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize 2026: Win USD 50,000 for Outstanding Achievements in STEM Research, Innovation, and International Cooperation
summary: Young scientists and innovators around the world now have an exceptional opportunity to gain international recognition for their contributions to science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). UNESCO has officially launched the call for nominations and applications for the third edition of the UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize for the Promotion of Young Scientists in STEM. This globally recognized biennial prize celebrates outstanding young researchers, innovators, educators, and scientific leaders whose work contributes to advancing scientific knowledge, strengthening STEM education, promoting innovation, and fostering international cooperation in support of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Five outstanding laureates from different regions of the world will each receive a monetary award of USD 50,000, in addition to a medal and diploma recognizing their achievements and contributions to society. Application Deadline Deadline: 30 November 2026 (Midnight UTC+1) Applicants and nominators are strongly encouraged to begin the application process early to allow sufficient time for account verification, nomination submission, and document preparation. About the UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize The UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize for the Promotion of Young Scientists in STEM was established to strengthen scientific research, improve STEM education, encourage innovation, and enhance international scientific cooperation. The Prize supports UNESCO s broader mission of promoting science as a driver of sustainable development and recognizes young scientists whose work addresses some of the world s most pressing challenges, including climate change, sustainable development, technological innovation, education, public health, environmental sustainability, and socio-economic advancement. Through this initiative, UNESCO seeks to inspire the next generation of scientists while encouraging governments, institutions, and communities to invest in scientific excellence and innovation. The Prize also places a strong emphasis on gender equality by encouraging the participation and recognition of women and girls in STEM disciplines. Prize Benefits Selected laureates will receive: Financial Award USD 50,000 for each laureate International Recognition UNESCO international recognition Global visibility among scientific communities Increased opportunities for collaboration and networking Enhanced professional credibility Official Honors UNESCO-Al Fozan Prize Medal UNESCO-Al Fozan Prize Diploma Career Advancement Opportunities Increased exposure to global scientific networks Opportunities for international partnerships Recognition from leading scientific institutions worldwide Number of Awards Available The Prize is awarded to five laureates every two years , representing the following geographical regions: Africa Arab States Asia and the Pacific Europe and North America Latin America and the Caribbean This regional representation ensures global inclusion and recognizes scientific excellence across diverse communities and research environments. Objectives of the Prize The UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize aims to: Promote excellence in STEM research Encourage innovation and scientific discovery Support STEM education and knowledge dissemination Strengthen international scientific cooperation Advance progress toward the Sustainable Development Goals Increase participation of women and girls in STEM Inspire future generations of scientists and innovators Who Can Apply? The Prize is open to young scientists, researchers, innovators, educators, and STEM professionals who have demonstrated exceptional contributions in their respective fields. Candidates must have achieved significant recognition for their work and demonstrate measurable impact within their area of expertise. Eligibility Criteria Age Requirement Applicants must: Be under 40 years of age on the application deadline date (30 November 2026) Achievement Requirement Candidates must have demonstrated significant achievements or contributions in one or more of the following areas: 1. STEM Research Including: Scientific research Applied research Emerging technologies Breakthrough discoveries Innovative scientific solutions 2. STEM Education and Innovation Including: STEM teaching and learning initiatives Science communication Dissemination of research findings Educational innovation Creativity and technological innovation 3. International or Regional Cooperation Including: Cross-border scientific collaboration Regional research partnerships International STEM initiatives Scientific diplomacy Knowledge-sharing programmes Focus on Gender Equality UNESCO strongly encourages nominations of young women scientists. The Prize seeks to address gender disparities in STEM by recognizing female scientific leaders and inspiring more girls and young women to pursue careers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. Application Process Applicants must complete an online application dossier. Step 1: Create an Online Account Candidates must: Register online Verify their email address Complete their profile information Step 2: Complete the Application Dossier Applicants must provide detailed information about: Educational background Professional achievements Scientific contributions Research impact Innovation activities International collaboration experience Step 3: Upload Supporting Documents Required documents include: Description of background and achievements Summary of research work and publications Evidence of significant contributions Statement explaining contribution to the Prize objectives Additional supporting documentation Step 4: Secure a Valid Nomination Applications are only valid if accompanied by an eligible nomination. Candidates must ensure their nominator submits the nomination before the application deadline. Step 5: Submit Before the Deadline Both the application and nomination must be completed by: 30 November 2026 (Midnight UTC+1) Nomination Requirements A nomination is mandatory. Applications submitted without an eligible nomination will not be considered. Who Can Nominate? Eligible nominators include: Government Institutions Governments of UNESCO Member States National Commissions for UNESCO UNESCO Partners UNESCO Chairs Category 2 Institutes and Centres under UNESCO Scientific Organizations International scientific unions Relevant scientific bodies Non-Governmental Organizations NGOs maintaining official partnerships with UNESCO Organizations active in STEM-related fields Universities Eligible university representatives include: Department Heads Deans University management officials Individual faculty members and staff members are not eligible to submit nominations independently. What Must a Nomination Include? A nomination should contain: Candidate Background Professional profile Educational background Major achievements Scientific Contributions Publications Research outputs Innovation achievements Major accomplishments Contribution to Prize Objectives Impact on STEM advancement Educational contributions Innovation and scientific leadership International cooperation efforts Important Application Notes Applicants should carefully note the following: Self-nominations alone are not accepted Applications must be completed in English or French Nominations must be completed in English or French Incomplete submissions will be rejected Late applications will not be accepted Account verification may require additional time Each nominating institution may nominate up to three candidates Supporting documents must be submitted before the deadline Why This Prize Matters The UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize is one of the world s most prestigious recognitions for emerging scientific leaders. Beyond the financial award, the Prize provides recipients with international visibility, global recognition, and access to influential scientific networks that can accelerate their careers and amplify the impact of their work. For young researchers and innovators dedicated to solving global challenges through science and technology, this Prize represents a powerful platform for showcasing excellence and contributing to sustainable development worldwide. How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Interested candidates should begin preparing their applications and securing eligible nominators as early as possible to ensure successful submission before the deadline. Final Thoughts The UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize for the Promotion of Young Scientists in STEM offers an extraordinary opportunity for young scientists, researchers, innovators, and educators to gain international recognition for their contributions to science and society. With five awards of USD 50,000 each, global visibility, and recognition from UNESCO, the Prize celebrates the next generation of scientific leaders working to advance knowledge, innovation, education, and sustainable development around the world. Eligible candidates are encouraged to prepare their applications and nominations well ahead of the 30 November 2026 deadline. Click here for more global award opportunities The post UNESCO-Al Fozan International Prize 2026: Win USD 50,000 for Outstanding Achievements in STEM Research, Innovation, and International Cooperation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: United Nations ESCAP Jobs 2026: 4 International Career Opportunities Open Across Asia-Pacific | Apply Before the Deadlines
summary: The United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP) has announced multiple international career opportunities for experienced professionals seeking to contribute to sustainable development, climate action, economic transformation, regional cooperation, and public administration across the Asia-Pacific region. These positions span senior leadership roles, programme management opportunities, and specialized consultancy assignments. Successful candidates will have the opportunity to work with one of the most influential regional bodies of the United Nations while helping member states achieve sustainable development goals and long-term economic resilience. Applications are currently open for four positions, with deadlines ranging from 8 June to 4 July 2026. About ESCAP The United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP) serves as the regional development arm of the United Nations Secretariat. ESCAP works to: Promote sustainable and inclusive economic growth Strengthen regional connectivity Support climate action initiatives Enhance policy development and implementation Facilitate technical cooperation among member states Advance the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Through policy research, technical assistance, capacity-building programmes, and regional cooperation initiatives, ESCAP supports countries in addressing emerging economic, environmental, and social challenges. 1. Head, Subregional Office for North and Central Asia (D-1) Location Almaty, Kazakhstan Application Deadline 27 June 2026 Position Overview ESCAP is seeking an experienced international development leader to head its Subregional Office for North and Central Asia (SONCA). The selected candidate will oversee ESCAP s engagement with: Armenia Azerbaijan Georgia Kazakhstan Kyrgyzstan Russian Federation Tajikistan Turkmenistan Uzbekistan As the organization s senior representative in the subregion, the successful candidate will lead policy dialogue, regional cooperation initiatives, stakeholder engagement, and strategic programme implementation. Key Responsibilities Represent ESCAP throughout North and Central Asia Lead subregional development programmes Support implementation of the Special Programme for Economies of Central Asia (SPECA) Provide policy advice to senior UN leadership Build partnerships with governments, development partners, civil society, and academia Manage budgets, staffing, and programme delivery Support regional sustainable development initiatives Qualifications Applicants should possess: Master s degree in Economics, Social Sciences, or a related field Minimum 15 years of progressively responsible professional experience Significant international-level experience Strong policy formulation and programme management expertise Experience working with governments and international organizations Why Apply? This role offers a unique opportunity to influence development policy and regional cooperation across one of the world s most strategically important regions. Click here for more and to apply 2. Programme Officer (P-3) Location Bangkok, Thailand Application Deadline 23 June 2026 Position Overview ESCAP is recruiting a Programme Officer to support the implementation of the Energy Transition for Green Growth and Prosperity Programme. This multi-year initiative focuses on accelerating sustainable energy transitions, promoting green industrial development, and supporting economic transformation throughout Asia-Pacific countries. The successful candidate will work within the Financing for Development Section of the Macroeconomic Policy and Financing for Development Division. Key Responsibilities Support programme implementation and evaluation Coordinate technical assistance projects Conduct policy research and analysis Support high-level advisory groups and donor engagement Organize workshops and stakeholder consultations Prepare reports, studies, and policy papers Contribute to financing and energy transition initiatives Support regional cooperation programmes Areas of Focus The role will contribute to work related to: Energy transition financing Green growth Sustainable development Regional cooperation Power grid interconnection Social development Artificial intelligence and economic transformation Macroeconomic policy Qualifications Applicants should have: Master s degree in Finance, Economics, Energy, International Relations, Business Administration, Political Science, or related fields Minimum five years of relevant professional experience Experience in financing energy transition projects Experience supporting international development initiatives Strong research and analytical capabilities Why Apply? This is an excellent opportunity for mid-career professionals interested in sustainable finance, energy transition, and international development. Click here for more and to apply 3. Director of Administration (D-1) Location Bangkok, Thailand Application Deadline 04 July 2026 Position Overview ESCAP is seeking a highly experienced executive leader to serve as Director of Administration. The position oversees all major administrative operations supporting ESCAP and various UN entities across the Asia-Pacific region. This is one of the most senior administrative leadership positions within ESCAP. Key Responsibilities The Director will lead operations covering: Human resources management Finance and budgeting Procurement Information technology services Conference services Real estate management Central support services Travel and transport services Host country relations The role also includes supporting major UN reform initiatives, modernization programmes, operational efficiency projects, and organizational transformation efforts. Qualifications Applicants must possess: Master s degree in Public Administration, Business Administration, Finance, Human Resources, Management, or related fields Minimum 15 years of progressively responsible administrative experience Experience leading large multi-functional teams Strong knowledge of UN systems and procedures Expertise in organizational transformation and operational management Why Apply? This position offers an opportunity to shape administrative excellence and operational innovation across one of the UN s largest regional commissions. Click here for more and to apply 4. Expert in Transport Finance (Consultant) Duty Station Bangkok, Thailand Work Location Fiji Application Deadline 08 June 2026 Contract Duration 22 June 2026 – 31 December 2026 Position Overview ESCAP is recruiting a national consultant to support the development of Fiji s Nationally Determined Contributions (NDC) 3.0 Costed Implementation Plan (CIP). The consultancy forms part of ESCAP s regional project aimed at strengthening member states capacity to implement low-carbon and climate-resilient transport systems. The assignment will help transform Fiji s climate commitments into actionable and financially viable transport investments. Project Goals The consultant will contribute to: Low-carbon transport development Climate-resilient transport planning Sustainable transport financing Climate mitigation strategies Green transport investment pipelines NDC implementation Key Responsibilities Sector Analysis Analyze Fiji s transport mitigation targets Review transport decarbonization strategies Assess policy priorities Project Pipeline Development Identify transport mitigation projects Estimate CAPEX and OPEX requirements Assess investment gaps Prioritize climate investments Policy and Technical Support Draft transport chapters for the Costed Implementation Plan Develop monitoring and reporting frameworks Integrate climate and gender considerations Stakeholder Engagement Participate in consultations and workshops Present technical findings Support validation processes Qualifications Applicants should possess: Advanced degree in Transport, Civil Engineering, Environmental Science, Climate Change, Energy Studies, or related fields At least seven years of experience in: Sustainable transport Climate change mitigation Transport decarbonization Transport finance Transport policy analysis Knowledge of Fiji s climate policies and institutional framework Strong expertise in transport modelling and GHG accounting Why Apply? This consultancy provides a unique opportunity to directly contribute to Fiji s climate transition strategy and support sustainable transport investments with long-term environmental and economic impact. Click here for more and to apply Important Application Deadlines Applicants should carefully note the following deadlines: Expert in Transport Finance – 08 June 2026 Programme Officer (P-3) – 23 June 2026 Head, Subregional Office for North and Central Asia (D-1) – 27 June 2026 Director of Administration (D-1) – 04 July 2026 Interested candidates are strongly encouraged to submit applications well before the closing dates. Why Consider a Career with ESCAP? Working with ESCAP provides professionals with the opportunity to: Influence regional and global development policy Collaborate with governments and international organizations Support climate action and sustainable development initiatives Build experience within the United Nations system Contribute to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals Work in diverse multicultural environments Lead impactful projects affecting millions of people across Asia and the Pacific For professionals seeking meaningful international careers, these ESCAP vacancies represent exceptional opportunities to create lasting impact while advancing their careers within the United Nations system. For more global job opportunities click here The post United Nations ESCAP Jobs 2026: 4 International Career Opportunities Open Across Asia-Pacific | Apply Before the Deadlines appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Oxford University Accelerator Fellowship Programme 2026: Funding and Support for High-Impact Ethical AI Projects
summary: Applications are now open for the prestigious Accelerator Fellowship Programme at the Institute for Ethics in AI, University of Oxford. Designed to advance responsible and ethical artificial intelligence, this pioneering fellowship supports individuals and teams working on impactful projects that can influence policy, industry, public understanding, and the future direction of AI. The programme is seeking 3–4 fellows for its current cohort and offers a unique opportunity to collaborate with one of the world s leading centres for AI ethics research. About the Accelerator Fellowship Programme The Accelerator Fellowship Programme is a flagship initiative of the Oxford Institute for Ethics in AI. The programme was created in response to the growing ethical challenges posed by the rapid advancement and adoption of artificial intelligence technologies across society. Unlike traditional research fellowships that focus primarily on academic outputs, this programme emphasizes real-world impact. It seeks to support projects that can shape the development of AI in ethical and socially beneficial directions while extending the influence of research already taking place within the Institute. The fellowship is grounded in: Philosophical inquiry Academic independence Interdisciplinary collaboration Practical impact Responsible innovation Through this approach, Oxford aims to bridge the gap between cutting-edge AI ethics research and meaningful implementation in society. Fellowship Objectives The Accelerator Fellowship Programme supports work that: Creates significant impact beyond academia Promotes the responsible and ethical development of AI Aligns with one or more research priorities of the Institute for Ethics in AI Amplifies and extends the impact of existing research at the Institute The programme encourages fellows to transform research insights into practical initiatives capable of influencing decision-making, industry practices, public understanding, and governance frameworks. Types of Projects Supported Oxford s Accelerator Fellowship Programme welcomes a diverse range of initiatives focused on ethical AI. Potential areas of impact include, but are not limited to: 1. AI Policy and Governance Innovation Projects that contribute to: AI regulation Governance frameworks Public policy development Ethical standards and accountability systems International cooperation on AI governance 2. Professional Development and Capacity Building Initiatives designed to: Train AI professionals in ethical practices Create educational opportunities Develop responsible AI leadership Build ethics competencies across sectors 3. Commercial and Technical Innovation Projects that support: Responsible AI products Ethical AI tools and systems Industry best practices Commercial solutions that prioritize fairness, transparency, and accountability 4. Strategic Networks and Partnerships Efforts focused on: Building alliances across sectors Connecting academia, industry, and government Facilitating interdisciplinary collaboration Creating sustainable AI ethics ecosystems 5. Public Engagement and Awareness Projects aimed at: Improving public understanding of AI Encouraging informed discussions about AI ethics Increasing public participation in AI-related decision-making Transforming public discourse around emerging technologies What Makes a Strong Application? The Institute has clearly stated that it is looking for projects that are already on a well-defined path toward impact. Applicants should note that this fellowship is not intended for: Early-stage concepts Idea-stage proposals Proof-of-concept projects Blue-sky research without implementation plans Instead, successful applicants are expected to demonstrate: Existing Project Development Projects should already be underway, with evidence of meaningful progress. Established Partnerships Applicants should have: Existing collaborators Strategic partners already engaged Clearly identified stakeholders who will support implementation Defined Delivery Roadmap Applications should include: Concrete milestones Realistic timelines Deliverables Implementation strategies Impact Measurement Framework Applicants must explain: How impact will be achieved How outcomes will be measured What indicators will be used to evaluate success Why This Fellowship Matters Artificial intelligence is increasingly shaping economies, governments, healthcare systems, education, security, and everyday life. As AI capabilities continue to expand, ethical considerations have become more important than ever. Questions around fairness, accountability, transparency, privacy, safety, and societal impact require practical solutions that go beyond academic discussion. The Accelerator Fellowship Programme seeks to bridge this gap by supporting leaders who can transform ethical AI research into actionable solutions that benefit society. Fellowship Benefits Although detailed funding information is provided within the fellowship s expanded sections, participants can generally expect: Affiliation with the University of Oxford s Institute for Ethics in AI Access to world-class expertise and networks Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration Support in accelerating project impact Increased visibility for ethical AI initiatives Engagement with leading researchers, policymakers, and industry practitioners Number of Fellowships Available The Institute for Ethics in AI is seeking to recruit: 3–4 Accelerator Fellows for the current cohort. Given the limited number of positions available, applicants are encouraged to prepare strong, evidence-based proposals demonstrating both readiness and potential impact. Application Process Interested applicants should: Review the official eligibility criteria carefully. Ensure their project aligns with at least one Institute research priority. Demonstrate existing project development and partnerships. Prepare a clear impact strategy and implementation roadmap. Submit their application through the official Oxford Institute for Ethics in AI channels. Apply Here Important Contact Information For fellowship-related enquiries: Email: aiethicsafp@philosophy.ox.ac.uk For general enquiries: Email: aiethicscomms@philosophy.ox.ac.uk Final Thoughts The Oxford Accelerator Fellowship Programme represents a unique opportunity for researchers, innovators, policymakers, entrepreneurs, and practitioners working at the intersection of artificial intelligence and ethics. By supporting projects that are already positioned for impact, the programme aims to accelerate real-world solutions that promote responsible AI development, strengthen governance frameworks, build strategic collaborations, and improve public understanding of AI. For individuals and teams committed to ensuring that artificial intelligence develops in ways that are ethical, inclusive, and socially beneficial, this fellowship offers access to one of the world s most influential platforms for AI ethics leadership. For more information on this fellowship visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY here. The post Oxford University Accelerator Fellowship Programme 2026: Funding and Support for High-Impact Ethical AI Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Scholars Incubator Program 2026: Global Research Fellowship for Emerging Scholars in MENA Studies by Social Sciences Lab MENA
summary: The Scholars Incubator Program offered by the Social Sciences Lab MENA (SSL MENA) is a prestigious international research development initiative designed to support, mentor, and empower emerging scholars in the fields of social sciences and MENA-focused research. This program is not just a short-term training course—it is a structured academic incubation ecosystem that provides junior researchers, graduate students, and early-career scholars with the institutional support, mentorship, and research environment needed to advance their academic careers on a global scale. Based in Europe and focused on the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region, SSL MENA is an independent, non-governmental, and non-partisan research institution that fosters interdisciplinary collaboration and academic excellence. The Scholars Incubator Program is one of its flagship initiatives, designed to bridge the gap between early academic potential and global research opportunities. About Social Sciences Lab MENA (SSL MENA) The Social Sciences Lab MENA is a European-based independent research institution dedicated to building a global scholarly community focused on MENA studies. The Lab brings together researchers from different academic disciplines, ideologies, and regions to encourage collaboration, knowledge exchange, and high-quality research output. Its core mission includes: Developing interdisciplinary research networks focused on the MENA region Supporting emerging scholars in academic publishing and funding applications Providing institutional affiliation to researchers worldwide Facilitating field research and research support services Connecting scholars across senior and junior academic levels Promoting independent research and intellectual freedom Unlike traditional institutions, SSL MENA does not impose a fixed research agenda. Instead, it empowers scholars to pursue their own research interests with institutional guidance and support. What is the Scholars Incubator Program? The Scholars Incubator Program is a structured academic development fellowship designed to help emerging researchers strengthen their academic identity, research capabilities, and publication potential. It provides participants with: Academic mentorship and supervision Research collaboration opportunities Institutional affiliation with a European research institute Publishing and funding application support Access to academic networks and research communities The program is designed to be both intellectually rigorous and professionally supportive, ensuring scholars develop practical research skills while advancing their academic careers. Program Vision and Philosophy The Scholars Incubator Program is built on the belief that researchers should have intellectual freedom, structured support, and collaborative opportunities. Key principles include: 1. Researcher-Centered Approach Participants are encouraged to develop independent research ideas rather than follow a fixed institutional agenda. 2. Inclusive Academic Environment The program welcomes scholars from diverse backgrounds, ideologies, and academic traditions, provided they adhere to research ethics and academic standards. 3. Interdisciplinary Collaboration Scholars from different disciplines work together to enrich research perspectives and outcomes. 4. Long-Term Academic Growth The program is designed not just for short-term training but for long-term academic career development. Key Benefits of the Scholars Incubator Program Participants in the program gain access to a wide range of academic, professional, and institutional benefits. 1. Research Affiliation One-year affiliation with the Social Sciences Lab MENA Possible extension through continued engagement with the Lab Community Recognition of academic participation and training 2. Mentorship and Academic Supervision Personalized mentorship from experienced scholars Guidance on research design and methodology Support in developing academic career pathways 3. Publishing and Academic Writing Support Participants receive: Guidance on academic publishing processes Editing and proofreading support Assistance in preparing journal submissions Feedback on research manuscripts 4. Funding and Career Development Support The program provides structured guidance for: Applying to research grants and fellowships European funding schemes such as: ERC (European Research Council) Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions Erasmus+ programs Research position applications across academic institutions 5. Access to Research Resources Scholars benefit from: Access to research data and institutional resources Participation in collaborative research projects Fieldwork and methodological support Integration into thematic research groups 6. Networking and Academic Community Participants join a global scholarly network that includes: Junior and senior researchers Principal investigators Guest scholars and experts Interdisciplinary academic communities This network enables long-term academic collaboration and professional development. 7. Certification and Institutional Recognition Participants receive: Affiliation certificate from SSL MENA Recognition of training participation Support letters when required for academic applications Program Structure and Learning Experience The Scholars Incubator Program is structured to provide a balanced academic and practical experience. Core Activities Include: 1. Mentorship Sessions 30 minutes monthly (individual sessions) Focused academic guidance and progress review 2. Monthly Group Sessions 1-hour collaborative academic discussions Peer learning and research exchange 3. Workshops and Opportunities Sessions 1-hour monthly training sessions Focus on research tools, publishing, and funding applications 4. Focus Zone Sessions 2-hour intensive group research discussions Project development and methodological support 5. Research Groups (Bonus Component) Participation based on availability of senior scholars and principal investigators Collaborative interdisciplinary research projects Research Environment and Academic Philosophy SSL MENA emphasizes flexibility and intellectual independence while maintaining academic rigor. Key features include: No fixed daily schedule for activities Flexible online participation model Weekend-focused sessions to accommodate global participants Clear deadlines for specific tasks Strong emphasis on peer collaboration and academic excellence Post-Program Opportunities and Long-Term Support One of the strongest aspects of the program is its continued support after completion. Participants gain access to the Lab Community , which provides: Continued research collaboration opportunities Access to funding calls and grant databases Integration into research projects and teams Ongoing mentorship and academic support Opportunities to publish and present research Career development across global academic networks This ensures that scholars continue to grow beyond the formal program duration. Eligibility Criteria To participate in the Scholars Incubator Program, applicants must meet the following requirements: Academic Requirements Minimum Bachelor’s degree (BA or equivalent) Graduated students or early-career scholars preferred Language Requirements Proficiency in Arabic is required English proficiency is considered an advantage Technical Requirements Reliable internet access for online participation Ability to engage in virtual meetings and collaborative sessions Professional Requirements Strong interest in academic research Commitment to participating in structured activities Willingness to collaborate with peers and senior scholars Geographic Eligibility Open to nationals of MENA countries Also open to international researchers with interest in MENA studies Application Process Applicants are required to complete the following steps: Step 1: Application Submission Complete the official application form via the program portal Step 2: Program Fee Pay a €100 program participation fee Important Note Admission is based on a first-come, first-served basis Early application is strongly recommended due to limited spots Program Duration and Timeline One-year research affiliation Program activities are ongoing with structured milestones Possible extension through Lab Community engagement Why You Should Apply The Scholars Incubator Program offers a rare opportunity for emerging scholars to gain institutional affiliation, mentorship, and research development support within a globally connected academic environment. Unlike traditional academic programs, it prioritizes independent research, interdisciplinary collaboration, and long-term academic growth. For researchers seeking to publish, secure funding, and build a strong academic career in MENA studies or social sciences more broadly, this program provides a powerful platform for development and international exposure. Apply Here For more information, visit the official website Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Scholars Incubator Program 2026: Global Research Fellowship for Emerging Scholars in MENA Studies by Social Sciences Lab MENA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: University of Helsinki RESDOC 2026: Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Research Programme in Finland with €2,900 Monthly Salary, International Mobility, MSCA COFUND Funding, and World-Class Research Training
summary: The University of Helsinki has launched applications for the prestigious RESDOC Doctoral Training Programme 2026, a fully funded international doctoral research initiative designed to train the next generation of researchers capable of addressing some of the world s most pressing sustainability, health, environmental, and societal challenges. Co-funded by the European Union through the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) COFUND Programme under Horizon Europe and the University of Helsinki, RESDOC offers selected doctoral researchers a unique opportunity to pursue a four-year fully funded doctoral degree in Finland while receiving structured research training, international collaboration opportunities, interdisciplinary supervision, and competitive employment conditions. Applications are open from 1 June 2026 to 31 August 2026. Application Deadline 31 August 2026 (11:59 PM Finnish Time EET) What is the RESDOC Programme? RESDOC (Training Doctoral Excellence for a Resilient and Just Future) is an innovative doctoral training programme established at the University of Helsinki to prepare researchers capable of generating solutions for complex global challenges. The programme is anchored within the University s major research profiling initiative, Resilient and Just Systems (RESET), and combines: High-quality doctoral research International collaboration Interdisciplinary training Industry engagement Public-sector partnerships Career development support Research mobility opportunities Successful candidates will become salaried doctoral researchers employed by the University of Helsinki while pursuing their doctoral degree. Why Choose RESDOC? RESDOC goes beyond traditional doctoral education by combining scientific excellence with transferable skills training and real-world engagement. Participants will benefit from: Full-time employment for four years Monthly gross salary of approximately €2,900 World-class supervision International research exposure Industry and public-sector collaborations Career development training Interdisciplinary research opportunities Access to one of Europe s leading research universities Structured doctoral training aligned with European standards Research Focus and Environment One of the most distinctive features of RESDOC is its interdisciplinary approach. Doctoral researchers are free to develop their own research topics provided they align with one of the programme s four major thematic areas. The Four RESDOC Research Themes 1. Infectious Diseases and Antimicrobial Resistance in Changing Environments Research under this theme focuses on: Emerging infectious diseases Public health threats Antimicrobial resistance Environmental drivers of disease Global health resilience Disease surveillance systems 2. Biodiversity and Nature s Resilience Researchers may explore: Ecosystem sustainability Biodiversity conservation Climate adaptation Ecological resilience Environmental restoration Nature-based solutions 3. Secure and Sustainable Food and Water Systems Potential topics include: Food security Sustainable agriculture Water governance Resource management Food systems transformation Agricultural resilience 4. Just Socio-Ecological Transition This theme addresses: Climate justice Social sustainability Environmental governance Green transitions Policy innovation Equitable development Outstanding Research Environment Doctoral researchers will conduct their studies within one of the University of Helsinki s 14 doctoral programmes while simultaneously participating in the interdisciplinary RESDOC community. This dual structure provides: Strong disciplinary expertise Cross-disciplinary collaboration Access to international networks Advanced research infrastructure Academic mentoring Professional development opportunities Supervision and Academic Support Every RESDOC doctoral researcher receives extensive academic support throughout the programme. Each candidate is assigned: Primary Supervisor A principal investigator (PI) from the University of Helsinki responsible for guiding the doctoral research. Co-Supervisor An additional supervisor who may come from: The University of Helsinki Industry partners Public sector organizations International institutions Thesis Committee Every doctoral researcher receives support from a Thesis Committee that monitors research progress and provides guidance. Peer Mentoring Groups Researchers participate in peer support groups that encourage collaboration, networking, and community building. Doctoral Studies and Training The doctoral degree combines original research with structured coursework. Degree Structure The programme consists of: Doctoral dissertation Mandatory studies totaling 30 ECTS credits RESDOC contributes approximately: 20–25 ECTS credits through specialized training modules Additional credits are earned through discipline-specific doctoral programme courses. RESDOC Training Components Doctoral Base Camp During the first year, participants attend an immersive retreat featuring: Expert workshops Research skills training Interdisciplinary discussions Networking opportunities Community building activities Annual Symposia Every year researchers participate in symposia involving: Supervisors Industry partners Associated organizations International experts Researchers Day Doctoral researchers organize annual thematic events focused on: Systems thinking Sustainability challenges Scientific collaboration Cross-disciplinary learning Artificial Intelligence Training Participants complete mandatory AI training covering: Responsible AI use Research applications Critical assessment of AI tools Innovation and Entrepreneurship Module Training includes: Innovation development Entrepreneurial thinking Commercialization skills Industry engagement Citizen Science Assignments Researchers engage communities through: Public participation projects Science communication Citizen engagement activities Compulsory International and Intersectoral Secondments A defining feature of RESDOC is its compulsory secondment programme. Each doctoral researcher completes a: Two- to four-month secondment These placements may occur in: Industry Government organizations Research institutions International organizations Benefits include: Practical experience Career networking Knowledge transfer Exposure beyond academia Importantly, doctoral researchers continue receiving their salary during secondments. Extensive International Partnership Network RESDOC collaborates with 23 associated partner organizations across academia, government, and industry. Partners include: Private Sector Orion UPM Kymmene Oyj Valio Raisio Group Resistomap Vetcare Demos Helsinki Public Sector City of Helsinki City of Lahti Finnish Environment Institute (SYKE) VTT HUS Diagnostics Natural Resources Institute Finland International Universities University of Nairobi University College Dublin University of Göttingen University of Turku Linnaeus University Swedish University of Agricultural Sciences Employment and Working Conditions Successful applicants become employees of the University of Helsinki. Employment Duration Four years 2027–2030 Salary Doctoral researchers receive: Approximately €2,900 gross per month Additional Benefits Employees enjoy: Occupational healthcare Social security coverage Paid annual leave Flexible working arrangements Professional development support Safe and inclusive working environment Living in Finland Selected researchers will relocate to Finland and reside there throughout the programme. Benefits of living in Finland include: High quality of life Excellent public services Safe society Strong research ecosystem International academic environment Nature-rich surroundings English-friendly environment Equality, Diversity and Inclusion RESDOC follows the principles of the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions and strongly promotes: Equal opportunities Diversity Inclusion Accessibility Non-discrimination The programme also supports researchers at risk whose academic freedom or career opportunities may be limited in their home countries. RESDOC Framework and Funding RESDOC is funded through one of Europe s most prestigious research funding mechanisms. Funding Source The programme is co-funded by: The European Union Horizon Europe Programme Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) COFUND University of Helsinki Why MSCA Funding Matters MSCA is widely recognized as the European Union s flagship programme for researcher development. Since 1996, MSCA has supported more than 150,000 researchers worldwide through: International mobility Research excellence Career development Cross-sector collaboration RESDOC operates under Grant Agreement No. 101261796. How to Apply Applications must be submitted through the University of Helsinki s online recruitment system. Step 1: Verify Eligibility Applicants must: Hold a Master s degree Not hold a PhD Have less than four years of research experience Meet English language requirements Meet mobility requirements Be willing to live in Finland Step 2: Prepare Required Documents Applicants must submit: Europass CV Motivation Letter and Doctoral Study Plan Degree Certificate and Transcript Proof of English Language Proficiency Research Proposal, Ethics Self-Assessment and Mobility Plan Step 3: Submit Application Applications must be submitted no later than: 31 August 2026 at 11:59 PM EET Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. Selection Process Timeline June–August 2026 Application period September 2026 Eligibility screening and evaluation October 2026 Academic alignment interviews November 2026 Final selection and supervisor matching December 2026 Employment preparation and relocation guidance January–February 2027 Programme commencement Final Thoughts The University of Helsinki RESDOC Doctoral Programme represents one of the most attractive fully funded doctoral opportunities currently available in Europe. Combining a competitive salary, international research training, interdisciplinary collaboration, world-class supervision, and extensive industry engagement, RESDOC offers exceptional preparation for future careers in academia, government, international organizations, and industry. Researchers passionate about sustainability, health, biodiversity, food systems, climate action, and social transformation are strongly encouraged to apply before the 31 August 2026 deadline. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post University of Helsinki RESDOC 2026: Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Research Programme in Finland with €2,900 Monthly Salary, International Mobility, MSCA COFUND Funding, and World-Class Research Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
